## 2026-07-05, Library Version 2026.07.135, PR #647

AI-access ISO mapping reconciliation (TODO 3.24, worker-QA FIT-4): the compliance matrix's AI Access and Agent Permissions Standard row is corrected from `A.8.3` (dropping `A.5.17`) to `A.5.15, A.5.16, A.5.17, A.5.18, A.8.2`, judged via ad-hoc `/matrix-fit` against the ISO/IEC 27002:2022 titles and matching the document's own table; the document-index ISO summary is aligned; plus the batched #646 QA rows and the TODO 3.24 rotation.

### Fixed

- **TODO 3.24 (worker-QA FIT-4): AI-access ISO mapping discrepancy.** [`compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md`](../../compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md) (`1.11.9` to `1.11.10`) mapped the AI Access and Agent Permissions Standard ([`ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md)) to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 `A.5.15, A.5.16, A.5.18, A.8.3` (skipping `A.5.17`, carrying `A.8.3` Information access restriction), while the standard's own section-11 framework-alignment table (line 259) claims `A.5.15 to A.5.18, A.8.2` with Relevance "Access control and privileged access". Routed through the ad-hoc `/matrix-fit` cadence (TODO 3.24 is its named trigger case: a control-code citation in doubt on the sensitive single-file matrix). The six candidate titles were verified against ISO/IEC 27002:2022 (`A.5.15` Access control, `A.5.16` Identity management, `A.5.17` Authentication information, `A.5.18` Access rights, `A.8.2` Privileged access rights, `A.8.3` Information access restriction). Judgment: the standard's scope is agent PERMISSIONS and privileged access (it extends the IAM policy and relates to the privileged-access-management standard), so `A.8.2` (Privileged access rights) is the correct A.8 control, not `A.8.3` (Information access restriction, a secondary AI-to-data fit the document author did not select), and `A.5.17` (Authentication information) fits the standard's identity-propagation and authentication scope and was wrongly dropped. The matrix row is corrected to `A.5.15, A.5.16, A.5.17, A.5.18, A.8.2`, matching the document's authoritative table (which needed no change).

### Changed

- [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.56` to `1.27.57`): the AI Access and Agent Permissions Standard's index ISO summary `A.5.15-18` is aligned to `A.5.15-18, A.8.2` (a third parallel surface carrying the document's ISO mapping; it did not carry the `A.8.3` error but omitted the `A.8.2` the document and now the matrix carry). Multi-surface check: the reverse crosswalk [`governance/matrix-reverse-framework-control-crosswalk.md`](../../governance/matrix-reverse-framework-control-crosswalk.md) does not reference this document; [`dev-security/standard-api-security.md`](../../dev-security/standard-api-security.md) co-occurs only as a Related-Document path link with its own unrelated ISO row.
- [`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml) and [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md) regenerated (taxonomy first) for the matrix and index Version bumps; [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md) regenerated byte-identical (no per-document version display for these rows), correctly absent from the diff.
- TODO 3.24 rotated to [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md). The scratch coverage sync for this TODO-item-set change is QUEUED for the consolidated Phase-1-boundary batch (advisory, cross-repo; not a CI gate).
- Batched per recursion-avoidance: the #646 `/validate-pr` row ([`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) `1.2.424`, 0 findings) and the #646 `/retro` row ([`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) `1.0.370`, the inline-path-in-root-CHANGELOG observation).

### Verification

- Substantive-tier change (a control-code semantic-fit correction on the sensitive compliance matrix): the six ISO control titles were verified against ISO/IEC 27002:2022 (not memory), the mapping judged via ad-hoc `/matrix-fit` against those titles and the document's scope, and the diff reviewed by a refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push. Gate 49 (matrix control-code existence) confirms `A.5.17` and `A.8.2` are valid 2022 codes; all 66 gates standalone post-commit; both generators `--check` clean after the Version bumps; [`tools/preflight-changelog.py`](../../tools/preflight-changelog.py) green pre-commit; [`tools/pre-push-guard.sh`](../../tools/pre-push-guard.sh) STANDALONE and UNPIPED before push.

## 2026-07-05, Library Version 2026.07.134, PR #646

Patch-vs-vulnerability SLA reconciliation (TODO 3.23, maintainer decision B2): patch-management's "Standard Critical" row is split to mirror the vulnerability-management source (PoC 72h / no-known-exploitation 7d), the section-7.4 metric generalized to stay coherent, plus the batched #645 QA rows and the TODO 3.23 rotation.

### Fixed

- **TODO 3.23 (worker-QA FIT-3): patch-vs-vulnerability SLA conflict.** [`operations/procedure-patch-management.md`](../../operations/procedure-patch-management.md)'s section-1 deployment-timeline table had a single "Standard Critical" row (`CVSS >= 9.0; not yet actively exploited`, 72 hours), while [`security/procedure-vulnerability-management.md`](../../security/procedure-vulnerability-management.md)'s section-2 table splits Critical into publicly-disclosed-with-PoC (72 hours) and no-known-active-exploitation (7 days); patch-management's Purpose (line 21) states it operationalizes the vulnerability-management SLAs, so the conflation gave an operator two answers for a no-known-exploitation Critical. Per maintainer decision B2, the row is split into two "Standard Critical" rows (publicly disclosed with a PoC available: 72 hours; no known active exploitation or public PoC: 7 days) mirroring the source. The classification NAME stays "Standard Critical", so the section-3 authorization-pathway table (one pathway) and the classification are unaffected. ## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.129, PR #641

Gate 66, the unbalanced-fence audit (the 3.15 GR-4 residual's maintainer-chosen standalone-check half): the shared fence-aware iterator every fence-skipping linter uses treats each fence line as a state toggle, so a file with an ODD fence-line count leaves the iterator inside a code block for the remainder, silently suppressing every downstream linter's scan of everything after the unclosed fence; the new [`tools/lint-unbalanced-fences.py`](tools/lint-unbalanced-fences.py) mirrors the iterator's toggle semantics exactly (one shared toggle for backtick and tilde fences), scans every markdown file outside the default-exempt trees, and fails on an odd count, reporting the last unclosed opener. Wired across the four parity surfaces with the section-6 prose pair and a section-5 category-5 clause (audit-spec `1.16.52` to `1.16.53`); three fixture tests (suite 334 to 337); the build-time corpus census found ZERO unbalanced fences (397 files), so the gate is preventive. Gate count 65 to 66; the pack's guardrail-review growth narrative advances in step (pack `1.54.1` to `1.54.2` with the paired version-history row). Carries the #640 QA batch per recursion-avoidance (1 Low, the verifier-fold audit-trail gap, fixed in this diff's detailed-mirror completeness clause) and the handoff/lease reconcile. Gate 60's drift threshold fired on this branch (the third consecutive threshold firing: gate 66 plus the claim-fit pair), so the `/guardrails` r5 review rides in this PR per the r4 precedent: three post-r4-delta lens subagents returned 14 items (none dropped), 5 fixed in this diff (the matrix-fit reciprocal claim-fit See-Also under the same pack `1.54.2` bump; the gate-54 docstring's stale "separate follow-up" clause; the gate-66 docstring's explicit-paths honesty residual; a `/claim-fit` pointer in [`governance/specification-citation-verification.md`](governance/specification-citation-verification.md) `1.2.8` whose coverage narrative predated the instrument; a garbled D7 comment), 4 routed to TODO 3.15 (led by the tilde-blind fence-predicate copies, Medium), 1 protected-touch bundle bullet (led by a live CLAUDE.md longer-check-in self-contradiction, Medium, the maintainer's reword call), 3 adjudications recorded; guardrail-history `1.0.6`, resetting the gate-60 baseline at inventory 66/12/18/11.

### Added

- [`tools/lint-unbalanced-fences.py`](../../tools/lint-unbalanced-fences.py): gate 66. Detection mirrors `lint_common.iter_non_code_lines` exactly (stripped-prefix match on three backticks or three tildes, one shared toggle), so a passing gate and a fully-scanned file are the same statement; scope is every `*.md` outside `DEFAULT_EXEMPT_DIRS` (the population the fence-aware linters collectively scan). Odd count fails with the last unclosed opener's line number.
- Three fixture tests (`UnbalancedFenceTests`): balanced-pass, unclosed-backtick fail, stray-tilde fail (the GR-4 motivating shape). Suite 334 to 337.

### Changed

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.122, PR #634

Delta gate D7, the handoff-snapshot freshness check (the first P3 build of the maintainer's 2026-07-04 re-tier, and the mechanization of the append-not-reconcile class after SEVEN logged occurrences the prose checklist line did not extinguish): when a PR touches [`.working/session-handoff.md`](../session-handoff.md), the new [`tools/check-handoff-snapshot-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-handoff-snapshot-on-pr.py) requires every labelled backtick version token on the Current-truth snapshot line (library CalVer, README, pack, audit-spec, runbook, guardrail-history, and the validate-pr / improvement-log / claim-fit history registers) to equal the named surface's live header at the PR head, and fails a duplicated labelled token (the seventh occurrence's shape). Delta-scoped by design: the snapshot legitimately lags merged `main` by one PR (recursion-avoidance), so the invariant is snapshot-fresh-at-refresh-time, and refresh time is exactly a handoff-touching PR; version tokens only, prose claims stay convention-guarded. Wired into the PR-time runner, the CI workflow, the parity registry's delta-step set, and audit-programme section 6.1 (table row + narrative, spec 1.16.51); six fixture tests (suite 332, one pinning the surface-table path-existence class); the guard header's and CLAUDE.md's delta-gate range refreshed to D1-D7 under the pre-authorized factual one-liner class, and the check earned its keep before shipping: its first parse of the real line forced two honest reconciles in this diff (the handoff's rides-next-PR annotation replaced with the tree's actual register values; the audit-spec token advanced with the spec bump), and its first committed dogfood run then FAILED on the gate's own confabulated guardrail-history live path (singular for plural), self-caught with a third snapshot reconcile (a stale suite count on the same line) and fixed by amend before push. Also carries the #633 QA batch (the charter's PIPEDA breach trigger corrected to the Act's "real risk of significant harm", the corpus's sole material-risk carrier; the #632 history-row b1 cell aligned to MOOTED) and the shipped bullet's rotation to the DONE ledger.

### Added

- [`tools/check-handoff-snapshot-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-handoff-snapshot-on-pr.py): delta gate D7. Trigger: the handoff in the PR diff. Check: each of nine labelled token patterns, when present on the Current-truth line, must match the named live file's header version at the PR head; duplicate labelled tokens, an unresolvable label (file or field missing), and a touched handoff with no Current-truth line all fail loud; absent labels are skipped (the line is prose, not a form). Deliberately no opt-out trailer (a refreshed snapshot with stale tokens has no legitimate case; an untouched handoff never triggers).
- Six fixture tests (`HandoffSnapshotOnPrTests`, five on the shared two-commit delta-gate harness plus one real-repo check): reconciled-pass, stale-token fail, duplicate-token fail, untouched-not-triggered, missing-snapshot-line fail, and a SURFACES path-existence test asserting every labelled live path resolves in the real repo with a parsable header field (pinning the confabulated-path class the build itself produced; see Discipline observations). Suite 326 to 332.

### Changed

- Wiring: [`tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh`](../../tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh) (D7 block + header list), [`.github/workflows/quality.yml`](../../.github/workflows/quality.yml) (the `Handoff-snapshot freshness check` step, PR-only), [`tools/lint-audit-gate-parity.py`](../../tools/lint-audit-gate-parity.py) (`WORKFLOW_DELTA_GATE_STEPS` gains the step name), [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) `1.16.50` to `1.16.51` (section 6.1 table row + per-check narrative).
- [`tools/pre-push-guard.sh`](../../tools/pre-push-guard.sh) header and [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md) PR-workflow step 1: the delta-gate range D1-D6 refreshed to D1-D7 (the pre-authorized factual one-liner class, the #623 precedent pair).
- [`.working/session-handoff.md`](../session-handoff.md): the Current-truth line reconciled to the D7-honest form (the rides-next-PR annotation replaced with the tree's actual register values `1.2.412` / `1.0.358`; the audit-spec token advanced to `1.16.51` with the spec bump), the check's own discipline applied to its first live subject.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): the section-3.15 snapshot-check bullet DELETED (rotated to [`DONE.md`](../DONE.md)).
- The #633 QA batch rides per recursion-avoidance: [`.working/validate-pr/2026-07-04-PR-633.md`](../validate-pr/2026-07-04-PR-633.md) + history `1.2.412`; [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) `1.0.358` (the #633 retro row: append-not-reconcile at SEVEN, the pass-through class at two, the consolidated recount clause staged).

### Discipline observations

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.119, PR #631

FR-59 privacy jurisdiction annex operational deepening, batch PR 1, and the first external-collaborator worker delivery applied from the scratch inbox: the Japan, United States, Canada, and Brazil privacy annexes deepen to operational level with the executable mechanics adopters run to, grounded in the primary texts and upstream-confirmed current at apply time. [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-japan.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-japan.md) gains an operational-requirements section on the consolidated APPI numbering (the Article 26 breach report and individual notification with the PPC-rules deadline caveat, the Articles 33 to 35 data-subject requests on the Act's "without delay" standard, Article 22 accuracy and deletion); [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-united-states.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-united-states.md) gains the cross-state operational baseline citable to the three earliest comprehensive state laws (the 45-day consumer-request clock with one 45-day extension, the Virginia and Colorado appeal duties, the data protection assessment triggers); [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-canada.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-canada.md) gains the PIPEDA section 10.1 breach-of-security-safeguards report and notification standard and the section 8(3) thirty-day access clock; [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md) gains the LGPD Articles 18 to 19 two-track data-subject-request clocks (immediate simplified form or 15-day complete declaration), the Article 37 records-of-processing obligation, and the ANPD's restructuring as a regulatory agency by Lei No. 15.352/2026. The Latin America annex correction-plus-deepening (including its stale Mexico entry) follows as batch PR 2 from the same delivery. The pre-push verifier round produced five fixes before push, headlined by a Critical catch: the 72-hour CAI breach clock the corpus attributes to Quebec Law 25 is a confabulation (the statute's standard is prompt notification with no fixed hour-count, confirmed against both the statute and the live consolidated Act); the two Canada-annex carriers are corrected here and the remaining five-document sweep is routed as new TODO item 1.10. Also carries the #630 QA batch (both in-window items fixed here) and the P2-boundary hallucination-metrics refresh, which opens the external-worker delivery row.

**Worker provenance:** [`inbox/worker-20260703-a/fr-59-privacy-jurisdiction/MANIFEST.md`](../../../grc_library_scratch/inbox/worker-20260703-a/fr-59-privacy-jurisdiction/MANIFEST.md) (research delivery: gap analysis, cited primary-source provisions, and proposed edit shapes; the orchestrator re-verified every relied-on quote in the primary texts and authored all final prose per the research-assistant discipline).

### Added

- [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-japan.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-japan.md) `1.0.4` to `1.1.0`: new `## Operational requirements` section. Article numbers follow the current consolidated APPI (the June-2020 amended-Act translation numbering differs; the consolidated numbering was confirmed against the official English translation this session): Article 26 breach report and individual notification (deadlines and thresholds live in the PPC Enforcement Rules, flagged confirm-before-encoding), Articles 33 to 35 data-subject requests ("without delay", no fixed day-count), Article 22 accuracy and deletion.
- [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-united-states.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-united-states.md) `1.1.5` to `1.2.0`: new `## Operational requirements (state comprehensive laws)` section. The 45-day response clock extendable once by 45 days (Cal. Civ. Code s. 1798.130(a)(2)(A); Va. Code s. 59.1-577(B)(1); Colo. Rev. Stat. s. 6-1-1306), the appeal duties (Virginia 60 days, s. 59.1-577(C); Colorado 45 plus 60 days, s. 6-1-1306; the CCPA/CPRA carries no equivalent), and the data protection assessments (Va. Code s. 59.1-580; Colo. Rev. Stat. s. 6-1-1309), phrased as "among the statutes' enumerated triggers" to avoid over-claiming the trigger lists.
- [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-canada.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-canada.md) `1.0.4` to `1.1.0`: new `## Operational requirements (PIPEDA)` section. Section 10.1 breach of security safeguards (real-risk-of-significant-harm threshold, report and individual notification as soon as feasible, no fixed hour-count, with Quebec Law 25's CAI notification noted as likewise a promptness standard) and the section 8(3) thirty-day access-request clock with the extension caveat. The s. 10.3 record-keeping duration was OMITTED: the commonly-cited 24-month figure could not be verified in the statutory text, so it is not asserted (regulation-level detail, the same confirm-before-encoding shape as Japan's PPC-rules caveat).
- [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md) `1.0.5` to `1.1.0`: two new obligation bullets, the Articles 18 to 19 data-subject-request tracks (immediate simplified form or complete declaration within 15 days; other Article 18 rights carry no statutory day-count) and the Article 37 records-of-processing obligation (the GDPR Article 30 analogue).

### Changed

- [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-brazil.md): the regulator line re-described per Lei No. 15.352/2026 (converting Medida Provisória No. 1.317/2025, which amended LGPD Articles 5 and 55-A): the ANPD is restructured as the Agência Nacional de Proteção de Dados, a regulatory agency, superseding the annex's "established as an independent federal authority" description. Upstream-confirmed this session (Planalto was 503; confirmed via the conversion record of MP 1.317/2025, scope limited to Articles 5 and 55-A, so Articles 18/19/37 as cited are unamended).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): section 2.1 gains the batch-PR-1 progress note (four annexes deepened; remaining: the Latin America batch PR 2 and the 18 source-gated annexes awaiting maintainer source drops); both P1-intro cadence enumerations gain `/claim-fit` (the routed #630 cross-reference finding, the enumeration the #630 ship itself widened; zero live residuals of the old three-item form confirmed by repo-wide grep).
- [`.working/hallucination-metrics.md`](../hallucination-metrics.md): the P2-boundary refresh (the r4 cadence note): the current-state header re-anchored to #630, the external-collaborator worker-delivery row OPENED (1 applied, zero apply-time hallucination catches; the flag-not-fabricate currency caveat resolved by orchestrator upstream confirmation), and the #626 three-layer intake catches folded into the catches log (four line-offsets, two counts, one verifier-caught inherited imprecision; the validation-layer pass-through class on second-occurrence watch).
- Generated artefacts regenerated in order (taxonomy first, then portal and scorecard) after the four Version bumps.

### Fixed

- The routed #630 `/validate-pr` c1 note: [`.working/claim-fit/history.md`](../claim-fit/history.md) `1.0.1` to `1.0.2`, the Summary cell's count-label granularity corrected to "six fixes across seven carrier locations" (and its "sixth carrier" parenthetical to "sixth fix"); the run record's "SIXTH carrier" clause aligned to fix granularity with the line-granularity count named.
- The five pre-push verifier round-1 findings, all validated then fixed in this diff:
  1. **Critical, Quebec 72-hour confabulation**: the new Canada breach bullet's contrast clause and the annex's pre-existing Quebec Law 25 line both asserted a 72-hour CAI notification clock; the statute (CQLR P-39.1, s. 3.5) requires prompt notification with no fixed hour-count, confirmed against the statute ("must promptly notify") and the live consolidated Act at LegisQuebec (zero "72 hours" occurrences). Both carriers reworded to the promptness standard; the seven remaining carriers in five untouched documents routed as TODO item 1.10 (single-session sweep, always-split).
  2. **Medium, Japan Article 22 obligation strength**: "must be kept accurate" overstated the statute's endeavour duty ("shall strive to"); reworded to the strive-to standard with the operationalization note.
  3. **Medium, US heading misnesting**: the new H2 had been inserted before the pre-existing `### Sectoral AI considerations` H3, orphaning it under the wrong parent; the new section relocated to after the sectoral block, restoring the outline.
  4. **Low, granularity-label class-width residual**: two surviving "sixth carrier"-at-fix-granularity carriers (the run record's fixes-list item 6; the #630 retro row's "caught a SIXTH" clause) aligned to the corrected fix/location labelling, the same class as the routed c1 note (fixed at class width, not only the cited instances).
  5. **Low, Virginia DPA timing**: "before processing" was attributed to both statutes but is held-text-grounded only for Colorado; reworded so the before-processing bar names Colorado alone (with the Virginia appeal nit folded in: the Attorney General channel is denial-conditioned in Virginia, any-outcome-with-concerns in Colorado).

### Verification

- Upstream currency confirmations, per the reference-version-currency rule's step 2 (the worker delivered all citations currency-UNCONFIRMED by design): Japan consolidated APPI numbering against the official Japanese Law Translation page; PIPEDA consolidation current to 2026-05-26 (Justice Laws); LGPD Articles 18/19/37 unamended through Lei No. 15.352/2026 (MP 1.317/2025 conversion, scope Articles 5 and 55-A only); CCPA 45-day clock intact through the 2026-01-01 amendments; VCDPA and Colorado CPA response and appeal clocks current as of 2026-07-04 against the official code sites.
- Every relied-on quote re-verified verbatim against the primary sources before authoring (APPI, CCPA, VCDPA, Colorado CPA, PIPEDA, LGPD); [`tools/lint-language.py`](../../tools/lint-language.py) clean on the four edited annexes; [`tools/preflight-changelog.py`](../../tools/preflight-changelog.py) green pre-commit; [`tools/run_all_audits.sh`](../../tools/run_all_audits.sh) standalone post-commit with direct exit capture (a first run caught the detailed-mirror #630 header dropped by the entry-insertion edit, restored and re-run to 65/65).

Round 2 confirmed all five fixes at class width and returned 1 new Medium: the routed 1.10 item undercounted its own enumeration ("six" remaining carriers where the enumeration and the verifier's independent corpus grep both give seven); fixed deterministically on both carrying surfaces to the verifier's empirically-derived figures (nine total across six documents; seven remaining across five untouched documents), with proximity-form contradiction greps in both orders confirming zero stale-count residue. This is the THIRD occurrence of the count/label granularity class the #630 retro put on watch; recorded for the #631 retro's pattern cell. [`tools/pre-push-guard.sh`](../../tools/pre-push-guard.sh) STANDALONE and UNPIPED before push.

Four-valued verdict vocabulary (`prescribed` / `informed-not-prescribed` / `mis-attributed` / `source-not-held`) because the fix differs by verdict class: a rephrased attribution (value stands), a corrected value or citation (per-finding, authorial choices surfaced), or a source-drop queue entry (an un-held source is never adjudicated from memory, per the evidence-grounded-completion external-version corollary and DD-10). `derives_from` [`evidence-grounded-completion.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/governance/evidence-grounded-completion.md).

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.115, PR #627

Gate 65, the cross-file section-name audit (the names phase, closing the two-PR plan whose numbers phase shipped as gate 62), WITH the `/guardrails` r4 review that its wiring's gate-60 threshold fires. The new [`tools/lint-cross-file-section-names.py`](../../tools/lint-cross-file-section-names.py) implements the title-anchoring rule from the same-day corpus survey: a parenthetical or quoted candidate after a cross-file reference is a title claim only when it exactly equals some numbered-heading title in the linked target, so a claim anchoring to a DIFFERENT number fails (the stale-renumber escape class) while the corpus's explanatory parentheticals and deliberate shorthands anchor to nothing and are skipped. Two evidence-backed deltas from gate 62 (table rows scanned, where the canonical renumber carriers live; the pack README exempt by relative path) and one seam the r4 overlap lens caught at build time (on table rows, an anchored title whose cited number vanished from the target now fails here, since gate 62 excludes tables); nine fixtures, suite 326; corpus-wide test-first run green. The r4 review (auto-fired at drift 3, gates 62 to 65) returned 19 findings across the three lenses: 10 fixed in-window, two of them partial with their carried halves on the GR-8(b) worklist (the gate's own seam and band notes; gate 64's section-6 scoping and boundary reword; the D6 boundary sentence; the GR-GAP-1 current-or-superseded boundary clause; the GR-8(b) pending-candidate enumeration with the dropped 1.9(c) line restored; the tests/README fixture-authoring rail; the runbook's delivery-time seed-removal and ledger-union reconciliations), 8 routed as seven section-3.15 r4 bullets, 1 cadence note (a hallucination-metrics refresh at the P2 applies boundary); the pack guardrail-review narrative advances to sixty-five (pack README 1.53.21, paired row). Also carries the #626 QA batch (2 no-action notes) and rotates the closed names-phase bullet to the DONE ledger.

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.112, PR #624

Gate-18 trailing-link seam fix (the r3 O-F1 guardrails finding, the second section-3.15 build-only pick of the overnight run): [`tools/lint-intra-doc-refs.py`](../../tools/lint-intra-doc-refs.py)'s cross-doc filter checked only the PRECEDING 60-char window for a markdown-link signal, while gate 62 claims links on either side of a section reference, so a "see section 5.4 in [foo](foo.md)" line (link AFTER the reference) was claimed by the two gates with different resolution targets. The filter now also scans a TRAILING 60-char window for the same two signals (a `.md` name or a `](` link marker), mirroring gate 62's bidirectional adjacency, with a regression fixture (suite 313) and a docstring note naming the seam. Also adds four TODO items for the wave-6 ad-hoc briefs staged in scratch (DORA and NIS2 annex operational deepening; ETSI Securing-AI and MITRE ATLAS 2026.06 alignment maps), research-state notes on the four P6 items whose scoping research is now briefed or delivered, the #623 QA batch (1 low + 2 notes; the two fixed here: the "D6 candidate" orphan-check label relabelled to D7 after #623 shipped the pack-README check as D6, and the considerations-ledger delta-range clause updated; the third routed to the next authorized CLAUDE.md touch), and the closed seam bullet's rotation to the DONE ledger.

### Changed

- [`tools/lint-intra-doc-refs.py`](../../tools/lint-intra-doc-refs.py): `is_cross_doc_context()` extended with a trailing 60-char window scanning for `.md` and `](` after the reference (previously only the preceding window carried the link heuristic); the docstring rewritten to name the bidirectional adjacency and the r3 O-F1 seam it closes. The change is filter-WIDENING only (more references classified cross-doc, never fewer), so no existing corpus finding can be newly introduced by it; gate 18 green on the corpus before and after.
- [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py): new `test_trailing_link_cross_doc_ref_not_flagged` fixture in `IntraDocRefTests` (a section reference followed by a markdown link resolves as cross-doc, rc 0); suite 312 to 313.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): the section-3.15 O-F1 seam bullet removed (rotated to the DONE ledger); new items 2.12 and 2.13 (DORA and NIS2 annex operational deepening) and 3.16 and 3.17 (ETSI SAI and ATLAS 2026.06 alignment maps) for the wave-6 ad-hoc briefs staged in scratch per the briefs-before-items convention; research-state notes added to the P6 items 6.1, 6.2, 6.4, and 6.5 (6.1's three follow-on briefs staged; the 6.2, 6.4, and 6.5 scoping research DELIVERED to the scratch inbox this boundary, pending apply or maintainer decision) with unchanged BUILD gating.

### Fixed

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.111, PR #623

Delta gate D6, the pack-README version-history co-bump check (the first section-3.15 build-only pick of the 2026-07-04 overnight run): when a PR changes the pack README's `**Version:**` value, the same diff must add the matching `## Version history` row. Mechanizes the close-out checklist's paired-surface instance (a), until now convention-only; the new [`tools/check-pack-readme-cobump-on-pr.py`](tools/check-pack-readme-cobump-on-pr.py) is delta-scoped, self-silencing on unchanged Version values, and carries no opt-out trailer (the paired-surface rule is unconditional). Wired into the PR-time runner, the CI workflow, the parity registry's delta-step set, and the audit-programme section 6.1 (table row + narrative, spec 1.16.48); four fixture tests (suite 312); the guard header's D1-D5 range refreshed to D1-D6, the PR-time runner's header list extended, and the same range refreshed, under the pre-authorized factual one-liner class, in [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md). Also carries the #622 QA batch (1 low + 1 note in-window, both fixed here), the overnight-mode transition (the overnight file to in-flight with the maintainer's 2026-07-04 authorization scope), and the shipped D-check bullet's removal from TODO section 3.15.

### Added

- [`tools/check-pack-readme-cobump-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-pack-readme-cobump-on-pr.py): delta gate D6. Trigger: the pack README `**Version:**` value differs between merge-base and head; check: an added `| <new-version> |` history-table row in the same diff; a missing or unparsable head Version line fails loud; absent-at-head (a pack-removing fork) is not-triggered. Live-probed against the real #622 range (bump-with-row, OK) and #621 range (untriggered) before the fixtures were written.
- Four `PackReadmeCobumpOnPrTests` fixtures on the shared delta-gate harness in [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py) (bump-with-row passes, bump-without-row fails, unchanged-Version untriggered, untouched-file untriggered); suite 308 to 312.

## 2026-07-04, Library Version 2026.07.109, PR #621

S3 citation-precision instrument, PR A of two (TODO section 1.4; design decided 2026-07-03 by the maintainer from three named options: the matrix-fit pattern, not a CI gate, because claim precision is semantic and the ground truth lives in the scratch reference base CI cannot see). Ships the recall-oriented triage tool [`tools/audit-claim-precision.py`](../../tools/audit-claim-precision.py) (`audit-*` named so the gate machinery does not adopt it; never CI-wired; exits 0; inline self-test with 6 extractor cases): it extracts Tier-A value-attribution claims (a specific value tied to a named normative source in one clause, both orders, the FR-120 shape) and Tier-B soft-alignment claims, and reports each source family's held-state from the scratch indexes (optional input). First census: 11 Tier-A rows and 119 Tier-B rows (two pre-push verifier rounds widened the soft-alignment phrasing set with inflected and compliance-form attributions); early judge candidates (the three 7-year ISO/IEC 42001 plus EU AI Act retention carriers and a 24-hour GDPR Article 33(2) KPI) are recorded in the design entry, not adjudicated. PR B (the `/claim-fit` judging skill plus the one-time full Tier-A pass) remains queued in TODO section 1.4. This PR also carries the #620 QA batch (the `/validate-pr` record and history row, the `/retro` row, and the F1 mirror-count correction).

### Added

- [`tools/audit-claim-precision.py`](../../tools/audit-claim-precision.py): the S3 PR-A triage tool. Tier-A extraction covers both claim orders (value-then-attribution-then-source; source-then-normative-verb-then-value) with a same-clause window; Tier B covers the soft-alignment phrasings; the scratch checkout is optional input with explicit-path no-fallback (the audit-brief-freshness precedent); the summary line names the census totals even when a `--tier` filter narrows the display (a display-count bug caught and fixed during the build's own first run).

### Changed

- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) section 1.4: the S3 park is ended by the recorded design decision; PR A marked shipped; the open half is PR B (the `/claim-fit` skill plus the one-time Tier-A judging pass).
- [`.working/design-decisions.md`](../design-decisions.md): the "S3 citation-precision instrument" entry (why not a gate; the two-PR shape; the cadence; the early judge candidates recorded verbatim so the PR-B pass starts from them).

### Verification

- Tool self-test 6/6 green; full corpus run exits 0 with 11 Tier-A and 119 Tier-B rows; every named source family in the Tier-A set reads held against the scratch indexes.
- The early judge candidates are deliberately NOT fixed in this PR: adjudicating them is exactly the PR-B judging pass's job, and pre-judging them here would repeat the inferred-as-verified shape the instrument exists to prevent.
- All 63 audit gates pass standalone post-commit; the PR-time checks pass in the pre-push guard chain.

### Discipline observations

- The pre-push skeptical verifier ran two rounds. Round 1 returned 4 findings, all validated and fixed before push: (1) a stale "roughly two hundred" Tier-B figure in the tool docstring left over from the pre-exclusion probe census, corrected to the actual count; (2) a recall gap in the ATTRIB phrasing set (the inflected "aligns with" plus compliance-form and definitional attributions were missed on about a dozen real corpus lines), widened; (3) a missing leading word boundary letting embedded matches ("pro**per GDPR**") produce spurious Tier-B rows, fixed with `\b`; (4) a leftover blanket "Each is a `lint-*.py`..." sentence in TODO section 1.4 that no longer described the section's only open item after the not-a-gate decision, rescoped to the shipped gates. Round 2 confirmed 3 of 4 and caught one residual in fix 2 (bare "maintains/verifies compliance with" still outside the widened set); the anchor was dropped to `compliance with` and the three named carrier lines mechanically confirmed present in the census. The two widenings moved the Tier-B census from 100 to 119.
- The #620 QA batch rides this PR per recursion-avoidance: the `/validate-pr` record (2 low in-window findings: the stale mirror count fixed here as F1; the coverage-pairing miss fixed in scratch PR #64 as F2), the history row (1.2.399), and the `/retro` row (1.0.344).
- Worker-pipeline state in this window: the external worker completed ALL NINE wave-1 P2 briefs (fr-41 was the last, scratch PR #63); every delivery screened for write-scope compliance, ledger-reconciled where cumulative-ledger add/add conflicts arose, and merged scratch-side pending Opus-routed corpus applies.

## 2026-07-03, Library Version 2026.07.108, PR #620

RM-10 pipe-guardrail hardening, TODO section 1.9 parts (a) and (b) (maintainer-directed; six pipe-masking incidents to date). Ships (1) the PreToolUse Bash hook [`.claude/hooks/block-verification-pipes.py`](../../.claude/hooks/block-verification-pipes.py), wired in [`.claude/settings.json`](../../.claude/settings.json), which blocks any command piping a named verification command (`pre-push-guard.sh`, `run_all_audits.sh`, `run-pr-time-checks.sh`, `unittest`, `lint-*.py`, [`preflight-changelog.py`](../../tools/preflight-changelog.py)) into a truncating filter (`tail`, `head`, `grep`, `sed`, `awk`), fail-open on malformed payloads; (2) the sanctioned wrapper [`tools/tail-safe.sh`](../../tools/tail-safe.sh), which runs the wrapped command unpiped, prints the last N lines plus an explicit `EXIT=<code>` line, and exits with the real code; and (3) the pre-push guard's pipe self-defence in [`tools/pre-push-guard.sh`](../../tools/pre-push-guard.sh) (refuses piped stdout, exit 3, with the documented `PRE_PUSH_GUARD_ALLOW_PIPE=1` override). One documented deviation from the item's literal spec: the guard check is `[ -p /dev/stdout ]` rather than `[ -t 1 ]`, because a plain invocation's stdout in this execution environment is a harness capture FILE (probe-verified), so a tty test would refuse every sanctioned run while the pipe test refuses exactly the masking shape. Hooks load at session start, so the deliberate blocked invocation could not fire in this session; script-level validation is complete (hook self-test 10 blocked / 14 allowed, including the mention-not-execution class the verifier surfaced plus direct block, allow, and malformed-payload cases; wrapper self-test 3 checks; guard refusal, override, and wrapper-route runs), and the next session re-runs the deliberate piped invocation to record the hook firing. The guard regression fixture updates in step (suite 307 to 308: the exit-chain tests adopt the documented override, and a new test pins the refusal path). This PR also carries the #619 QA batch (the `/validate-pr` record and history row, the `/retro` row, and the handoff PROGRESS reconcile fixing the #619 sweep's F1).

### Added

- [`.claude/hooks/block-verification-pipes.py`](../../.claude/hooks/block-verification-pipes.py): PreToolUse Bash hook rejecting pipe-masked verification commands (exit 2 with the reason and the sanctioned alternatives; exit 0 otherwise; fail-open on malformed payloads by design, since a guardrail hook that could block all Bash on a parse error would be worse than the mistake it prevents). Segment-aware regex (fd redirects like `2>&1` stay in-segment; `;` ends a segment; `tail-safe.sh` invocations pass). Inline `--self-test` with 10 blocked and 14 allowed cases (the allowed set includes mention-not-execution pipelines: the command-position anchor passes `grep`/`cat`/`wc`/`git log` inspections of a lint file and quoted mentions, while executions still block).
- [`tools/tail-safe.sh`](../../tools/tail-safe.sh): the sanctioned display-truncation wrapper (`tail-safe.sh [-n LINES] -- command`), capturing to a temp file, printing the last N lines plus `EXIT=<code>`, exiting with the real code. Inline `--self-test` (success propagation and truncation, failure-code propagation, stderr capture) per the `audit-*` precedent, so the gate-36 regression runner does not adopt a non-linter tool.
- Hook wiring in [`.claude/settings.json`](../../.claude/settings.json) (PreToolUse, matcher `Bash`).

### Changed

- [`tools/pre-push-guard.sh`](../../tools/pre-push-guard.sh): pipe self-defence block at start (refuse `[ -p /dev/stdout ]` unless `PRE_PUSH_GUARD_ALLOW_PIPE=1`, exit 3, loud message naming the sanctioned paths). CI unaffected (it invokes the runners directly).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) section 1.9: parts (a) and (b) recorded shipped with the pipe-check deviation rationale and the next-session hook-firing validation residual; the section stays open on (c) (the CLAUDE.md widening on the next authorized touch).
- [`.working/session-handoff.md`](../session-handoff.md): the #618-era PROGRESS tail reconciled to historical form (the #619 `/validate-pr` F1 fix; one authoritative "Queue from here" remains) and the new-state PROGRESS updated for #620.

## 2026-07-03, Library Version 2026.07.107, PR #619

Worker-ready brief staging, slices 2, 3, and 5 (the build and the whole-backlog seeding). The scratch half shipped in [`grc_library_scratch`](https://github.com/jposluns/grc_library_scratch) as its PRs #50 through #53: the staged-brief convention, coverage index, and gate shape-checks (slice 2), then seeding in three priority-tier waves (slice 5), ending with every TODO item mapped to a staged worker-ready brief (13 briefs) or a recorded not-eligible verdict (48 coverage rows; brief drafting fanned out to in-session research subagents, two for wave 1 and four for waves 2 and 3, every draft orchestrator-verified before landing). The main-repo wiring (slice 3) ships here: the PR close-out checklist's worker-brief coverage-pairing line, the `/resume` step-3 staged-brief freshness check, and the advisory cross-repo freshness tool [`tools/audit-brief-freshness.py`](../../tools/audit-brief-freshness.py) (orchestrator-side, never a CI gate). [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) section 4.4 records the build state (slice 4, the `/subagent` entry command, remains) and section 4.2's stale premise line is corrected. The RM-10 incident count moves to six on both live carriers, covering a fifth display-only pipe and a sixth pipe-masked audit exit capture, both self-caught; these count refreshes and the pairing-line addition are the entry's [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md) touches, the counts under the maintainer's pre-authorized factual one-liner class and the pairing line as designed slice-3 scope.

### Added

- [`tools/audit-brief-freshness.py`](../../tools/audit-brief-freshness.py): the slice-3 advisory cross-repo freshness instrument. Reports (1) the scratch coverage index's stamp age in PRs-behind against the newest merged main PR (advisory threshold 15), (2) dead brief target paths (a staged brief naming a path no longer on `origin/main`), and (3) dead coverage-row TODO anchors (a row's section anchor with no live TODO heading). Named `audit-*` so the gate machinery does not adopt it; always exits 0; the scratch checkout is optional input (absent checkout is a no-op, not a failure); parser self-test inline behind `--self-test`.
- The worker-brief coverage-pairing bullet in the [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md) PR close-out checklist: a [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) item-set delta pairs with a queued-or-done scratch coverage sync (a verdict or brief per new item, removal per closed item, anchor updates per renumber).
- The `/resume` step-3 staged-brief freshness check in [`.claude/commands/resume.md`](../../.claude/commands/resume.md), queueing a targeted coverage-refresh sync when the advisory tool reports findings.

### Changed

- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) section 4.4: build state updated to slices 1, 2, 3, and 5 shipped, slice 4 remaining (the slice list collapses to the one open slice). Section 4.2: the stale "Python-focused guidance" premise line corrected (the pack's languages tree has shipped 11 baseline files including the decided JS/TS + Go + Java subset since the initial release; surfaced by a wave-2 seed worker, independently verified against `origin/main` before the edit).
- RM-10 incident count five to six on both live carriers ([`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md) PR-workflow step 2 and [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) section 1.9), adding the sixth incident: a post-commit full-audit run piped to `tail` with the exit code captured from the pipe (masking the audit's own exit), self-caught in this PR's build and re-run with a file-redirect exit capture. Proximity-form contradiction grep for stale five-incident phrasing: zero hits on live surfaces.

### Verification

- All 63 audit gates pass standalone post-commit (re-run unpiped with file-redirect exit capture after the sixth-incident catch; exit 0).
- The advisory tool's inline self-test passes (4 parser tests), and its live report against the landed scratch state is clean: 13 staged briefs checked, 48 coverage rows checked, index 0 PRs behind, 0 findings, independently corroborating the seeding waves.
- Scratch-side verification lived in the scratch PRs: wave-wide owned-path disjointness re-verified per wave (finally 60 owned paths across 13 briefs, 0 conflicts), the scratch `validate` gate green on every wave, placeholder contradiction greps clean.

### Discipline observations

- **Seeding worker fan-out and apply-time catches.** Four in-session research subagents drafted the wave-2/3 seeds (the wave-1 pair is part of the same seeding effort). Orchestrator verification of their drafts produced: the section-6.2 PQC disposition taken stricter-safe (source-gated verdict, FR-70 shape) against the worker's borderline-eligible lean; the section-4.2 stale-TODO-premise discovery (worker-flagged, orchestrator-verified, fixed here); the SR-5 ETSI mis-bucket claim initially misjudged by a too-shallow orchestrator grep and confirmed correct on the deeper catalogue read (the worker was right); and the EU-annex carve-out decision applied across the fr-59/fr-74 briefs at wave-1 landing.
- **Sixth RM-10 incident (self-caught).** The recurrence four commits after the count moved to five underlines the maintainer-accepted section-1.9 mechanical hardening (hook plus wrapper), which stays at the head of the queue after this staging series.
- **Push-before-guard deviation (self-reported).** The stop-hook committer-identity amend of the lease-refresh commit was pushed on the hook's instruction without the pre-push guard chain; the guard was run immediately after, standalone and unpiped, and passed (both runners green), so the pushed state was verified post-hoc. The deviation is recorded for the next `/retro`.

## 2026-07-03, Library Version 2026.07.101, PR #613

Tooling refactor (TODO section 3.15, the GR-3 metadata-parser migration, wave 2): the identical private Version-window parsers that gate 40 ([`tools/lint-version-bump-recency.py`](../../tools/lint-version-bump-recency.py)), delta check D2 ([`tools/check-version-bump-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-version-bump-on-pr.py)), and delta check D4 ([`tools/check-date-cobump-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-date-cobump-on-pr.py)) each carried (a 30-line head window plus a bold-marker Version regex, three copies) are retired in favour of one shared `head_version()` helper in [`tools/lint_common.py`](../../tools/lint_common.py), built on the wave-1 `parse_metadata_block()`: field precedence `Version` then `Library Version` (the README metadata `Version` alias is deliberately not read), an empty value treated as absent, and the head-window scope unchanged. D4's Date read moves onto the shared `parse_metadata_block()` plus `parse_iso_date()` pair, preserving its annotated-Date-reported-as-missing behaviour and removing a re-parse branch the pre-validated date parse makes unreachable (a calendar-invalid Date now folds into the missing-canonical-Date finding rather than drawing its own message; the failing exit is unchanged). The headline documented narrowing: the retired regex tolerated leading whitespace before the `**Version:**` marker; the shared parser is line-initial, which every canonical document satisfies. Three further edge-case divergences, each confirmed corpus-neutral (zero live instances), are recorded in the helper's docstring, and all three migrated checks were re-run against the real #612 diff with byte-identical result lines (gate 40: 336 versioned documents scanned; D2 and D4: 5 changed documents checked, with 6 exempt and 7 skipped in each). Three regression tests pin the helper's precedence, empty-value, and head-window semantics (suite 304 to 307). The TODO section-3.15 GR-3 bullet is trimmed to its wave-3 residual (a decision on the gate-1 and taxonomy-builder parsers, not a build). Batches the #612 [`/validate-pr`](../../.working/validate-pr/history.md) and [`/retro`](../../.working/improvement-log.md) rows plus both #612 sweep fixes per recursion-avoidance.

### Changed

- [`tools/lint_common.py`](../../tools/lint_common.py): new shared `head_version(text, *, head_lines=METADATA_HEAD_LINES)` helper. It parses the metadata head window via the wave-1 `parse_metadata_block()` and returns the document's `Version` field value, falling back to `Library Version` (the root README's CalVer line), with the README metadata `Version` alias deliberately out of scope and an empty field value returned as `None` (absent). The docstring records the precedence, the empty-as-absent rule, the wave-2 narrowing (line-initial field markers only; the retired per-tool regexes tolerated leading whitespace, which no canonical document uses), and three further corpus-neutral edge-case divergences (a no-separator value now parses; precedence is by field name rather than window position; an empty first Version line no longer falls through to a later one).
- [`tools/lint-version-bump-recency.py`](../../tools/lint-version-bump-recency.py) (gate 40): the private `METADATA_HEAD_LINES` / `VERSION_LINE_PATTERN` constants and `extract_version()` helper are removed; target discovery now asks the shared `head_version()`. The git-side pickaxe regex (`GIT_VERSION_REGEX`) is untouched: it matches diff lines, not head-window state, and stays local by design. The unused `re` import is dropped and the module docstring updated.
- [`tools/check-version-bump-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-version-bump-on-pr.py) (delta check D2): same constant/helper removal; the base- and head-ref Version reads go through `head_version()`. The pre-existing local variable named `head_version` was renamed `head_version_value` at every use (including the failure-message f-string) so the imported helper is not shadowed.
- [`tools/check-date-cobump-on-pr.py`](../../tools/check-date-cobump-on-pr.py) (delta check D4): Version reads migrate as in D2 (with the same shadow-avoiding rename); the Date read is rebuilt on `parse_metadata_block()` plus `parse_iso_date()`, preserving the check's annotated-Date-reported-as-missing behaviour (a `**Date:** 2026-07-03 (draft)` style value still fails as "no canonical Date"), and the `ValueError` re-parse branch the pre-validated parse makes unreachable is removed with an explanatory comment (pre-migration the branch was reachable only for a shape-valid, calendar-invalid Date such as 2026-02-30, which now folds into the missing-canonical-Date finding with the same failing exit).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): the section-3.15 GR-3 bullet is rewritten to its wave-3 residual, recording waves 1 and 2 as shipped and framing wave 3 as a DECISION (whether the gate-1 `extract_metadata` and the taxonomy builder's duplicate-field parser should also migrate) rather than a build.

### Added

- [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py): three `head_version()` tests in `LintCommonHelperTests` pinning (1) field precedence proper (an input carrying BOTH fields with `Library Version` listed first still returns the `Version` value, pinning name-precedence over position; a verifier-round addition), plus that a README-style metadata `Version` alias never matches and that `Library Version` with a trailing annotation parses; (2) empty, absent, and `None` inputs all returning `None`; and (3) the head-window scope (a Version line below the window is not read). Suite 304 to 307.

### Fixed

- Two #612 post-merge sweep findings, both in-window: the [`.working/DONE.md`](../../.working/DONE.md) #612 entry's fixture count corrected ("three fixtures" to "five fixtures", the L-1 finding), and the [`.working/design-decisions.md`](../../.working/design-decisions.md) pinned-design heading for the worker-provenance gate gains the BUILT/ACTIVATED addendum naming gate 50 check 3 and PR #612 (the I-1 finding).

### Verification

- Functional equivalence: all three migrated checks re-run against the real #612 diff (d8cb431 to d9f051f) with byte-identical result lines to the pre-migration runs (gate 40: "OK: 336 versioned document(s) scanned; all have a Version field bumped at or after their most-recent body change. 0 document(s) had no Version-line commit history (likely single-commit add)."; D2: "OK: 5 changed versioned document(s) have a bumped Version field. 6 exempt file(s), 7 non-versioned file(s) skipped."; D4: "OK: 5 document(s) bumped Version in this PR; each co-bumped its Date to the bump commit's date. 6 exempt file(s), 7 non-bumping file(s) skipped.").
- `python3 -m unittest` green (307 tests); `tools/run_all_audits.sh` all gates pass on the committed state. The pre-push guard (which chains the corpus runner and the PR-time delta runner against this PR's merge base) runs standalone and unpiped before push.
- The audit-spec narratives for gate 40, D2, and D4 were re-read: they describe field-level and git-side behaviour, not the retired private regexes, so no [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) change is needed.

### Discipline observation

- The derived-count timing rule from the #612 retro is applied here: the suite and result-line counts in this entry were computed from live runs after the change set stabilized, not drafted ahead of the verifier round.
- Addendum (the #613 post-merge sweep's L-1): the "failing exit is unchanged" equivalence claim in this entry and the D4 module comment overstated for one doubly-degenerate corner. The migration also moved the Date parse AHEAD of the defensive no-range-commit skip, so a calendar-invalid Date on a diff-listed file no range commit touched (a merge artefact) previously passed via the silent skip and now fails loud on the Date. Fail-loud direction; the comment was amended in the next PR.
- Skeptical-verifier round (substantive tier, refute-briefed): 1 Medium and 3 Low findings, all fixed pre-push. The Medium: the precedence test carried no both-fields input, so the precedence rule itself was unpinned (a both-fields assertion added). The Lows: the D4 module comment and the lead overstated Date-semantics equivalence for the calendar-invalid class (reworded, with the finding-text delta documented); three corpus-neutral edge-case divergences were undocumented beside the headline narrowing (added to the helper docstring and this entry); the quoted gate-40 result line was truncated (completed). The verifier live-executed old and new implementations byte-identically on the full corpus and the real #612 diff.

## 2026-07-03, Library Version 2026.07.100, PR #612

The multi-session codification's last deliverable and the second of the two maintainer-injected concurrency priorities, closing TODO §3.6 with the rotation to [`.working/DONE.md`](../../.working/DONE.md): gate 50's dormant worker-provenance check (its check 3) ACTIVATES, now that both of its documented preconditions exist. The external-collaborator worker primitive arrived with the scratch WORKER-ONBOARDING flow (a Model-B worker delivers research to the scratch repository's `inbox/<worker-id>/` with a manifest file), and this PR defines the marking convention: a PR that applies such a delivery carries a `**Worker provenance:**` line in its detailed-mirror CHANGELOG entry naming the delivery path. The check validates each marker line's shape (it must reference the `inbox/` path so the attestation is traceable), enforcing presence and well-formedness per the gate's honest-backstop framing; an UNMARKED worker application is free prose no gate can detect, guarded by a new close-out checklist line. Both #611 post-merge sweep findings are fixed in-window (the design record's stale BUILD-deferred framing gains a SHIPPED addendum recording both as-shipped deltas; the gate-63 linter now reports missing-field and value errors together in one run, with a tenth fixture). With #611 and this PR merged, the concurrency prerequisites for a second maintainer session are complete. The #611 QA trio (1 Low, 1 Informational, both fixed here) batches in per recursion-avoidance.

### Changed

- [`tools/lint-bookkeeping-parity.py`](../../tools/lint-bookkeeping-parity.py) (gate 50): check 3, worker-provenance attestation, activated from its documented dormant stub. `worker_provenance_findings()` parses [`CHANGELOG-detailed.md`](CHANGELOG-detailed.md) for `**Worker provenance:**` marker lines and flags any that do not reference an `inbox/<worker-id>/` delivery path; the module docstring's check-3 paragraph rewritten from stub to active; the OK line now reads `worker-provenance attestation` rather than `worker-provenance dormant`. Presence-not-correctness, unchanged framing.
- [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) to 1.16.47: the gate-50 detailed-prose third-check sentence rewritten from dormant stub to active, naming both satisfied preconditions and the convention.
- [`.working/multi-session-orchestration.md`](../../.working/multi-session-orchestration.md): the external-worker delivery paragraph gains the manifest-file shape and the marking convention.
- [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md): the PR close-out checklist gains the worker-provenance marker bullet (the unmarked-application residual is this checklist line's job).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): section 3.6 deleted (rotation to [`.working/DONE.md`](../../.working/DONE.md)); the P3 backlog-totals line recounted to 5 items; the three surviving forward references to the section (the section-2.11 pairing clause and the section-4.4 INPUT-half framing) reworded to cite the DONE ledger instead of the deleted heading.
- Five new check-3 fixtures in [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py) (well-formed marker; pathless marker flagged; no-marker prose out of scope; the bullet authoring form validated both ways; a value-less marker line flagged) plus the #611 sweep's combined-case fixture below. Suite grows to 304.

### Fixed

- **The #611 sweep's L-1**: the design record's session-concurrency section ([`design-decisions.md`](../design-decisions.md)) heading no longer claims "BUILD deferred to a later session"; a build-outcome note records the ship (PR #611), the three locked sub-decisions, and BOTH as-shipped deltas (the lease shipped active rather than as the released stub; acquire moved before orientation), and the sub-decisions heading no longer says "confirm at the implementing session".
- **The #611 sweep's I-1**: [`tools/lint-session-state.py`](../../tools/lint-session-state.py) `main()` now prints BOTH the missing-field errors and any coexisting value errors before exiting (exit-code precedence unchanged, 2 over 1), so the docstring's coexisting-defects claim holds across the missing/invalid class boundary; a tenth `SessionStateTests` fixture covers the combined case.

### Verification

All 63 audit gates pass standalone after the single consolidated commit (gate 50 exercised with its activated check against the live mirror; gate 60 at the designed drift-1 warning); the regression suite passes at 304 with the six new fixtures; both generators regenerated in order and check-clean. The mechanical run does not itself prove the marking convention's prose coherence across its four carriers (linter docstring, spec narrative, runbook, checklist bullet); that was verified by re-reading each carrier against the convention sentence after all edits landed. Pre-push, the substantive-tier skeptical verifier returned four findings, all validated, fixed, and mechanically re-verified before push: (F1, High) the runbook's body changed without a Version/Date co-bump, a deterministic D2-class blocker (bumped to 1.0.7 / 2026-07-03); (F2) the marker regex missed the mirror's natural list-bullet authoring form (now bullet-tolerant); (F3) the pinned-design entry the activated code cites still framed check 3 as dormant (activation notes added, the same stale-framing shape as the #611 L-1); (F4) the marker value could span onto the next line (now same-line-only, an empty remainder flags).

### Discipline observations

The session-concurrency lease took its first live REFRESH in this PR (heartbeat re-stamped, `Current-task` advanced), exercising the close-out step #611 shipped. The #611 sweep record and both QA rows batch in per recursion-avoidance; both sweep findings were fixed in-window here rather than deferred.

## 2026-07-03, Library Version 2026.07.99, PR #611

The session-concurrency interlock, the first of the two maintainer-injected concurrency priorities (maintainer-requested 2026-06-26; all three sub-decisions locked 2026-07-02: 60-minute staleness window, advisory HOLD, well-formedness gate in the same PR), closing TODO §3.7 with the rotation to [`.working/DONE.md`](../../.working/DONE.md). The new lease [`.working/session-state.md`](../../.working/session-state.md) declares which session holds the shared `main` state surfaces (ACQUIRE at `/resume`, heartbeat REFRESH at each PR close-out, RELEASE in the session-closing handoff PR), and the new `/resume` step 0 reads it from `main` plus a `git fetch` cross-check of unmerged `origin/claude/*` sibling branches BEFORE anything else, HOLDing and surfacing on a live signal rather than proceeding on a timeout. Gate 63 ([`tools/lint-session-state.py`](../../tools/lint-session-state.py)) guards the lease's shape (five required field lines, a valid status, a parseable UTC heartbeat, status/branch coherence), four-surface-wired with nine regression fixtures; the interlock DECISION lives in the `/resume` procedure because CI runs per-branch and cannot see across concurrent sessions. Honest scope, per the design record: an advisory interlock, not a hard mutex; it prevents the realistic accidental double-resume, not a determined simultaneous launch. The #610 QA trio (a zero-finding post-merge sweep) batches in per recursion-avoidance.

### Added

- [`tools/lint-session-state.py`](../../tools/lint-session-state.py) (gate 63, session-state lease audit): requires the five field lines (`Active-session`, `Status`, `Last-heartbeat-UTC`, `Current-task`, `Worker-dispatches`), a `Status` of `active` / `winding-down` / `released`, a `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` heartbeat, and status/branch coherence (`released` requires `Active-session: none`; the live statuses require a branch name). All three status values pass when coherent: the gate guards SHAPE only, one step purer than its lifecycle-file sibling gate 46 (which additionally fails the overnight file's valid `done` state as a needs-processing signal), because a malformed lease would silently disable the `/resume` step-0 procedure that makes the interlock decision. Each field line appears exactly once and the coherence check runs unconditionally, so coexisting defects all report in one run. Wired across all four surfaces ([`.github/workflows/quality.yml`](../../.github/workflows/quality.yml), [`tools/run_all_audits.sh`](../../tools/run_all_audits.sh), [`.pre-commit-config.yaml`](../../.pre-commit-config.yaml), and the [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) section-6 inventory row plus detailed-prose pair plus the section-5 freshness-and-lifecycle group).
- [`.working/session-state.md`](../../.working/session-state.md): the lease itself, shipped carrying THIS session's active lease (branch, `Status: active`, fresh heartbeat) rather than a released stub, because a released stub would be a false declaration while this session is live. The file documents the acquire/refresh/release lifecycle and the two-part interlock inline.
- `SessionStateTests` in [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py): nine fixtures (valid active lease; valid released lease; missing field; invalid status; malformed heartbeat; released-with-branch incoherence; active-with-none incoherence; duplicate field line; HEAD smoke test). Suite grows to 298.
- `/resume` step 0 in [`.claude/commands/resume.md`](../../.claude/commands/resume.md): the interlock procedure (lease read from `main`; the 60-minute staleness window; the advisory HOLD that never proceeds on a timeout; the git cross-check crash net; the abandoned-session takeover surface; the acquire-on-proceed write).

### Changed

- [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md): the PR close-out checklist gains the lease-heartbeat refresh bullet (lifecycle summary, gate-63 pointer, design-record pointer).
- [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) to 1.16.46: gate-63 row, detailed-prose pair, and section-5 group-7 clause.
- [`dev-security/claude-rules/skills/guardrail-review/SKILL.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/skills/guardrail-review/SKILL.md) growth narrative advanced to sixty-three (gate-39 keyed) with the pack README at [`dev-security/claude-rules/README.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/README.md) 1.53.17 and its paired history row.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): section 3.7 deleted (rotation to [`.working/DONE.md`](../../.working/DONE.md)); the P3 backlog-totals line recounted to 6 items.
- [`.working/session-handoff.md`](../../.working/session-handoff.md): PROGRESS block advanced through #610 with the injected-priority queue; the How-to-resume list gains the step-0 lease check.

### Verification

All 63 audit gates pass standalone after the single consolidated commit (gate 60 reads machinery drift 1 against the fresh r3 baseline, inside the threshold); the regression suite passes at 298 with the nine new fixtures; both generators regenerated in order (taxonomy first, then portal and scorecard) and check-clean. The gate-coverage limits mean the mechanical run does not itself prove the step-0 procedure's semantics; those were verified by walking the design record's four step-0 cases against the shipped prose (live lease, live sibling branch, released stale, abandoned takeover). Pre-push, the substantive-tier skeptical verifier ran on the full diff per the standing standard.

### Discipline observations

The lease ships ACTIVE rather than as a released stub (a deviation from the design record's build-plan step 1, recorded here and in the entry above): the capturing design assumed the implementing session would stub it, but a stub claiming `released` while this session is live would defeat the interlock the moment a second session resumed, so the truthful active lease is the stricter-safe shape.

### Discipline observations

The #599 sweep's first dispatch surfaced a NEW background-agent stall shape (documented in the #599 retro row): a consolidated diff overflowed to a persisted-output file outside the agent's sandbox, the follow-up read was permission-rejected, and the rejection's wait-for-user instruction halted the unattended agent. Detection was the standing 60/90-second liveness SOP; recovery was probe-then-RESUME (context preserved, the four verified steps not re-run), cheaper than the kill-and-redispatch the self-backgrounded-command shape requires. Sweep and verifier briefs now carry a rail: never read persisted-output paths outside the sandbox; re-run the command scoped smaller inside the repo.

- The #595 sweep's M-2: the audit spec's section-6.1 D5 narrative refreshed to the widened forms 3 and 6 ([`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) 1.16.41 to 1.16.42).

The research's four-level-depth caveat resolved itself mechanically: the PII gate reads any four-dotted clause token as an IPv4 address, so the corpus's clause depth is capped at three levels and the nested clause was promoted to a peer instead; the D5-narrative refresh also re-learned that this gate-scanned spec says "backlog" rather than the backlog file's literal name near mandatory verbs (the uncertainty-marker gate).

- [`compliance/policy-compliance-and-audit-management.md`](../../compliance/policy-compliance-and-audit-management.md): all four mis-titled MEA01 cells restored to the canonical COBIT 2019 practice titles verified against COBIT 2019: MEA01.01 "Establish governance" to "Establish a monitoring approach", MEA01.02 "Conduct assessments" to "Set performance and conformance targets", MEA01.04 "Report results" to "Analyze and report performance", MEA01.05 "Review and report" to "Ensure the implementation of corrective actions". The first and fourth are the #587 sweep's M-1 (the routed bullet named only two; the whole-table check found the other two, same class, same move-together constraint).
- ISO 31000 clause-number correction (the #587 pre-push verifier's addition): "§6.7 (Monitoring and review)" to §6.6 on the three carriers that move together, [`governance/procedure-grc-programme-management-and-annual-review.md`](../../governance/procedure-grc-programme-management-and-annual-review.md) (1.0.5 to 1.0.6, the alignment sentence and the framework table row) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (1.27.53 to 1.27.54, the row-77 alignment cell). Per ISO 31000:2018, 6.6 is Monitoring and review and 6.7 is Recording and reporting; the row content was 6.6 semantics, so the number was the defect. A corpus-wide hunt (the sweep re-ran it independently) found no further carriers.
- [`tools/lint-language.py`](../../tools/lint-language.py): a `VERBATIM_ENSURE_TITLES` span mask (currently one entry, the COBIT 2019 MEA01.05 practice title) applied to the `ensure that` check on both scan paths, per the house-style verbatim-quote exemption: a canonical source title must be reproduced exactly, not reworded to satisfy house style. And the supplier bullet's parenthetical mis-attributes the objective title to the practice code: BAI05, the objective, is Managed Organizational Change; BAI05.02 is Form an effective implementation team; the fit reasoning and the applied recode stand.

Two pre-push verifier catches were applied before push: (1) the exception-policy bullet's MEA01 suffix was the MEA domain name, not the objective title, the same non-canonical-title class as the four practice cells, fixed with its health-report sibling, after which the scoped re-verify swept the class corpus-wide and surfaced seven more carriers, canonicalized in the fourth commit (the verify-fix loop's third iteration; the class is now zero-residual corpus-wide); (2) the identical BAI05 supplier-context fit question in the due-diligence procedure, plus the two row-fit questions the canonical titles expose, were NOT silently recoded (no fit verdict exists for them) and are routed to a section-3.14 ad-hoc fit-pass bullet instead. The whole-table sweep on a fit or title verdict is now part of fit-pass routing (the #587 retro row carries the lesson). The gate-2 false positive on the canonical MEA01.05 title was fixed in the gate (a narrow, enumerated span mask), not by rewording the verbatim title or exempting a file; the first draft of the spec-narrative sentence itself tripped the rule it described and was reworded, the same self-referential trap the worked-example carve-outs exist for.
